transport.c(3) Library Functions Manual transport.c(3)

NAME

transport.c - WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64
Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). #define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512
Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). #define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING
#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Functions


acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char *name, const char *url, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket client transport. acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create (const char *name, struct lws *wsi, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection.

Detailed Description

WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

Implements the acip_transport_t interface for WebSocket connections. Enables browser clients to connect via WebSocket protocol.

ARCHITECTURE:

  • Uses libwebsockets for WebSocket protocol handling
  • Async libwebsockets callbacks bridge to sync recv() via ringbuffer
  • Thread-safe receive queue handles async message arrival
  • Same pattern as WebRTC transport for consistency

MESSAGE FLOW:

1.
send(): Synchronous write via lws_write()
2.
LWS callback: Async write to receive ringbuffer
3.
recv(): Blocking read from receive ringbuffer

MEMORY OWNERSHIP:

  • Transport OWNS wsi (WebSocket instance)
  • Receive queue owns buffered message data
  • recv() allocates message buffer, caller must free

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file websocket/transport.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64

Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). Set to 64 for balance: allows buffering across one large frame's fragments while keeping latency reasonable. With ~70 fragments per 291KB frame, we need at least 64 slots to avoid dropping the frame while it's fragmenting.

Definition at line 57 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512

Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). Set to 64 to match incoming queue and prevent "BINARY after FIN" errors. Large video frames fragment into ~70 chunks; queue must be large enough to buffer one complete message worth of fragments.

Definition at line 66 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING

Definition at line 69 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Definition at line 70 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

Function Documentation

acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char * name, const char * url, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)

Create WebSocket client transport.

Parameters

url WebSocket URL (e.g., "ws://localhost:27225")
crypto_ctx Optional encryption context (can be NULL)

Returns

Transport instance or NULL on failure

Definition at line 1315 of file websocket/transport.c.

1316                                                                                        {
1317   if (!name) {
1318     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1319     return NULL;
1320   }
1321 
1322   if (!url) {
1323     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "url is required");
1324     return NULL;
1325   }
1326 
1327   // Parse URL to extract host, port, and path
1328   // Format: ws://host:port/path or wss://host:port/path
1329   const char *protocol_end = strstr(url, "://");
1330   if (!protocol_end) {
1331     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid WebSocket URL format (missing ://)");
1332     return NULL;
1333   }
1334 
1335   bool use_ssl = (strncmp(url, "wss://", 6) == 0);
1336   const char *host_start = protocol_end + 3;
1337 
1338   // Find port (if specified)
1339   const char *port_start = strchr(host_start, ':');
1340   const char *path_start = strchr(host_start, '/');
1341 
1342   char host[256] = {0};
1343   int port = use_ssl ? 443 : 27226; // Default: wss:// uses 443, ws:// uses 27226 (ascii-chat WebSocket port)
1344   char path[256] = "/";
1345 
1346   if (port_start && (!path_start || port_start < path_start)) {
1347     // Port is specified
1348     size_t host_len = port_start - host_start;
1349     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1350       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1351       return NULL;
1352     }
1353     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1354     host[host_len] = '\0';
1355 
1356     // Extract port
1357     port = atoi(port_start + 1);
1358     if (port <= 0 || port > 65535) {
1359       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid port number");
1360       return NULL;
1361     }
1362   } else {
1363     // No port specified, use default
1364     size_t host_len = path_start ? (size_t)(path_start - host_start) : strlen(host_start);
1365     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1366       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1367       return NULL;
1368     }
1369     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1370     host[host_len] = '\0';
1371   }
1372 
1373   // Extract path
1374   if (path_start) {
1375     SAFE_STRNCPY(path, path_start, sizeof(path) - 1);
1376     path[sizeof(path) - 1] = '\0';
1377   }
1378 
1379   log_info("Connecting to WebSocket: %s (host=%s, port=%d, path=%s, ssl=%d)", url, host, port, path, use_ssl);
1380 
1381   // Allocate transport structure
1382   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1383   if (!transport) {
1384     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1385     return NULL;
1386   }
1387 
1388   // Allocate WebSocket-specific data
1389   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1390       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1391   if (!ws_data) {
1392     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1393     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1394     return NULL;
1395   }
1396 
1397   // Initialize destruction state flag (CALLOC zero-initializes, but explicit for clarity)
1398   atomic_store_bool(&ws_data->is_destroying, false);
1399 
1400   // Create receive queue
1401   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1402   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1403     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1404     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1405     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1406     return NULL;
1407   }
1408 
1409   // Create send queue for client transport to buffer outgoing messages
1410   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1411   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1412     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1413     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1414     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1415     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1416     return NULL;
1417   }
1418 
1419   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1420   char recv_name[64], state_name[64];
1421   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1422   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1423 
1424   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1425     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1426     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1427     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1428     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1429     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1430     return NULL;
1431   }
1432 
1433   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1434     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1435     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1436     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1437     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1438     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1439     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1440     return NULL;
1441   }
1442 
1443   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1444     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1445     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1446     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1447     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1448     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1449     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1450     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1451     return NULL;
1452   }
1453 
1454   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1455     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1456     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1457     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1458     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1459     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1460     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1461     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1462     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1463     return NULL;
1464   }
1465 
1466   // Allocate initial send buffer
1467   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = LWS_PRE + 524288; // Match tx_packet_size (512KB)
1468   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(ws_data->send_buffer_capacity, uint8_t *);
1469   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1470     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1471     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1472     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1473     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1474     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1475     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1476     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1477     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1478     return NULL;
1479   }
1480 
1481   // Create libwebsockets context
1482   // Protocol array must persist for lifetime of context - use static
1483   // IMPORTANT: "http" protocol MUST be first for WebSocket upgrade handshake to work
1484   static struct lws_protocols client_protocols[] = {
1485       {
1486           "http", // Required first for WebSocket upgrade
1487           websocket_callback,
1488           0,      // Per-session data (unused, using connect_info.userdata instead)
1489           524288, // RX buffer size
1490           0,      // ID
1491           NULL,   // User pointer (will be set from connect_info.userdata)
1492           524288  // TX packet size
1493       },
1494       {
1495           "acip", // ACIP protocol
1496           websocket_callback,
1497           0,      // Per-session data (unused, using connect_info.userdata instead)
1498           524288, // RX buffer size
1499           0,      // ID
1500           NULL,   // User pointer (will be set from connect_info.userdata)
1501           524288  // TX packet size
1502       },
1503       {NULL, NULL, 0, 0, 0, NULL, 0} // Terminator
1504   };
1505 
1506   // Disable client compression for now - causes assertion in lws_set_extension_option()
1507   // This is a known issue with libwebsockets permessage-deflate negotiation
1508   // Server-side compression is still enabled, data will be compressed from server to client
1509   // but client->server traffic remains uncompressed (acceptable since client sends less data)
1510   // static const struct lws_extension client_extensions[] = {
1511   //     {"permessage-deflate", lws_extension_callback_pm_deflate, "permessage-deflate; client_max_window_bits=15"},
1512   //     {NULL, NULL, NULL}};
1513 
1514   struct lws_context_creation_info info;
1515   memset(&info, 0, sizeof(info));
1516   info.port = CONTEXT_PORT_NO_LISTEN; // Client mode - no listening
1517   info.protocols = client_protocols;
1518   info.gid = (gid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1519   info.uid = (uid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1520   // Initialize SSL globally for client (required even for clients to use OpenSSL)
1521   // The LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT flag works for both servers and clients
1522   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT;
1523   info.extensions = NULL; // Disable client compression due to lws_set_extension_option() assertion
1524 
1525   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
1526   // Default is 4KB, causing 291KB frames to fragment into 73 × 4KB chunks
1527   // Increase to 512KB to match server and allow larger WebSocket frames without fragmentation
1528   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 512 * 1024; // 512KB per-thread service buffer
1529 
1530   // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
1531   // PING every 30 seconds if idle, close after 35 seconds total with no response
1532   static const lws_retry_bo_t client_keep_alive_policy = {
1533       .secs_since_valid_ping = 30,   // Send PING after 30s idle
1534       .secs_since_valid_hangup = 35, // Hangup if still idle after 35s
1535   };
1536   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &client_keep_alive_policy;
1537 
1538   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
1539   lws_log_init_client();
1540 
1541   ws_data->context = lws_create_context(&info);
1542 
1543   if (!ws_data->context) {
1544     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1545     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1546     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1547     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1548     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1549     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1550     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1551     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1552     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1553     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create libwebsockets context");
1554     return NULL;
1555   }
1556 
1557   // Connect to WebSocket server
1558   log_debug("Initiating WebSocket connection to %s:%d%s", host, port, path);
1559   struct lws_client_connect_info connect_info;
1560   memset(&connect_info, 0, sizeof(connect_info));
1561   connect_info.context = ws_data->context;
1562   connect_info.address = host;
1563   connect_info.port = port;
1564   connect_info.path = path;
1565   connect_info.host = host;
1566   connect_info.origin = host;
1567   connect_info.protocol = "acip";
1568   // Use SSL + skip server certificate hostname verification + allow self-signed certs (for development)
1569   connect_info.ssl_connection = use_ssl ? (LCCSCF_USE_SSL | LCCSCF_SKIP_SERVER_CERT_HOSTNAME_CHECK | LCCSCF_ALLOW_SELFSIGNED) : 0;
1570   connect_info.userdata = ws_data;
1571 
1572   log_debug("Calling lws_client_connect_via_info...");
1573   ws_data->wsi = lws_client_connect_via_info(&connect_info);
1574   log_debug("lws_client_connect_via_info returned: %p", (void *)ws_data->wsi);
1575   if (!ws_data->wsi) {
1576     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1577     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1578     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1579     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1580     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1581     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1582     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1583     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1584     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1585     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1586     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to connect to WebSocket server");
1587     return NULL;
1588   }
1589 
1590   ws_data->is_connected = false;      // Will be set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_ESTABLISHED
1591   ws_data->connection_failed = false; // Set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_CONNECTION_ERROR
1592   ws_data->owns_context = true;       // Client transport owns the context
1593 
1594   // Initialize transport
1595   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1596   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1597   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1598 
1599   // CRITICAL: Give libwebsockets time to initialize connection before starting service thread
1600   // libwebsockets needs to process the initial connection handshake callbacks before lws_service()
1601   // is called repeatedly. Concurrent access to context during connection setup can trigger assertions.
1602   // Sleep 50ms to let lws_client_connect_via_info() callbacks complete initialization.
1603   log_debug("Delaying service thread start to allow libwebsockets connection initialization...");
1604   platform_sleep_us(50000); // 50ms delay
1605 
1606   // Start service thread after connection initialization
1607   // Only the service thread should call lws_service() on this context
1608   log_debug("Starting WebSocket service thread...");
1609   ws_data->service_running = true;
1610   if (asciichat_thread_create(&ws_data->service_thread, "ws_service", websocket_service_thread, ws_data) != 0) {
1611     log_error("Failed to create WebSocket service thread");
1612     ws_data->service_running = false;
1613     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1614     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1615     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1616     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1617     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1618     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1619     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1620     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1621     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1622     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1623     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to create service thread");
1624     return NULL;
1625   }
1626   log_debug("WebSocket service thread started");
1627 
1628   // CRITICAL FIX: Do NOT block the main thread waiting for connection!
1629   // This prevents stdin/keyboard input from being processed and causes the client to hang.
1630   // The service thread will establish the connection asynchronously.
1631   // The protocol layer (recv) will detect if connection fails and handle it there.
1632   // Return immediately so the main thread can respond to keyboard input.
1633   log_debug("WebSocket transport created, service thread will establish connection asynchronously");
1634 
1635   // Register transport
1636   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1637 
1638   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1639   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1640 
1641   // Return transport immediately - connection will be established by service thread
1642   // If connection fails, recv() will detect is_connected=false and return error
1643   return transport;
1644 }




References asciichat_thread_create(),
    atomic_store_bool(), lws_log_init_client(),
    mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(), platform_sleep_us(),
    ringbuffer_create(), ringbuffer_destroy(),
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, and WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE.


Referenced by server_connection_establish(), and
    websocket_client_connect().






acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create
  (const char * name, struct lws * wsi, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)


Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection. Wraps
    an already-established libwebsockets connection (from server accept). Used
    by websocket_server module to create transports for incoming clients.




Parameters


wsi Established libwebsockets connection (not
  owned by transport)


crypto_ctx Optional crypto context


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on error


Definition at line 1656 of file
    websocket/transport.c.


1657                                                                                        {
1658   if (!name) {
1659     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1660     return NULL;
1661   }
1662 
1663   if (!wsi) {
1664     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid wsi parameter");
1665     return NULL;
1666   }
1667 
1668   // Allocate transport structure
1669   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1670   if (!transport) {
1671     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1672     return NULL;
1673   }
1674 
1675   // Allocate transport-specific data
1676   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1677       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1678   if (!ws_data) {
1679     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1680     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1681     return NULL;
1682   }
1683 
1684   // Initialize receive queue
1685   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1686   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1687     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1688     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1689     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1690     return NULL;
1691   }
1692 
1693   // Initialize send queue (for server-side transports)
1694   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1695   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1696     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1697     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1698     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1699     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1700     return NULL;
1701   }
1702 
1703   // Initialize pending-free queue for deferred buffer freeing
1704   // permessage-deflate compression holds buffer references asynchronously,
1705   // so we defer freeing to prevent use-after-free errors
1706   ws_data->pending_free_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(pending_free_item_t), 256);
1707   if (!ws_data->pending_free_queue) {
1708     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1709     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1710     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1711     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1712     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create pending-free queue");
1713     return NULL;
1714   }
1715 
1716   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1717   char recv_name[64], send_name[64], state_name[64], pending_free_name[64];
1718   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1719   snprintf(send_name, sizeof(send_name), "send_%s", name);
1720   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1721   snprintf(pending_free_name, sizeof(pending_free_name), "pending_free_%s", name);
1722 
1723   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1724     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1725     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1726     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1727     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1728     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1729     return NULL;
1730   }
1731 
1732   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1733     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1734     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1735     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1736     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1737     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1738     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1739     return NULL;
1740   }
1741 
1742   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->send_mutex, send_name) != 0) {
1743     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1744     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1745     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1746     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1747     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1748     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1749     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize send mutex");
1750     return NULL;
1751   }
1752 
1753   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1754     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1755     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1756     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1757     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1758     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1759     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1760     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1761     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1762     return NULL;
1763   }
1764 
1765   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1766     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1767     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1768     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1769     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1770     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1771     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1772     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1773     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1774     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1775     return NULL;
1776   }
1777 
1778   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->pending_free_mutex, pending_free_name) != 0) {
1779     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1780     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1781     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1782     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1783     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1784     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1785     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1786     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->pending_free_queue);
1787     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1788     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1789     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize pending-free mutex");
1790     return NULL;
1791   }
1792 
1793   // Allocate send buffer with LWS_PRE padding
1794   size_t initial_capacity = 4096 + LWS_PRE;
1795   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(initial_capacity, uint8_t *);
1796   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1797     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1798     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1799     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1800     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1801     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1802     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1803     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1804     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1805     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1806     return NULL;
1807   }
1808   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = initial_capacity;
1809 
1810   // Store connection info (server-side: no context ownership, connection already established)
1811   ws_data->wsi = wsi;
1812   ws_data->context = lws_get_context(wsi); // Get context from wsi (not owned)
1813   ws_data->owns_context = false;           // Server owns context, not transport
1814   ws_data->is_connected = true;            // Already connected (server-side)
1815   log_info("[WEBSOCKET_TRANSPORT_CREATE] ★★★ SERVER TRANSPORT CREATED: is_connected=true, wsi=%p, ws_data=%p",
1816            (void *)wsi, (void *)ws_data);
1817   log_debug("Server transport created: is_connected=true, wsi=%p", (void *)wsi);
1818 
1819   // Initialize transport
1820   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1821   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1822   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1823 
1824   log_info("Created WebSocket server transport (crypto: %s)", crypto_ctx ? "enabled" : "disabled");
1825 
1826   // Register transport
1827   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1828 
1829   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1830   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1831 
1832   return transport;
1833 }






References mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(),
    ringbuffer_create(), ringbuffer_destroy(),
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, and WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE.








Author


Generated automatically by Doxygen for ascii-chat from the source
    code.







  

    Version 0.9.0
    ascii-chat
